rationale: Both correctly land on the ground-truth disposition (duplicate of #4808, pointer to #4809) and give the same technical root-cause narrative. X is better grounded: it cites the specific gh API field ('mergedAt is null') indicating it actually queried PR state rather than assuming, and appropriately hedges its escalation as a request-for-confirmation rather than a flat assertion. Y asserts '(merged)' confidently in the body text but then contradicts itself with a footnote admitting merge-state was 'not verified via gh this run,' which is a real honesty/grounding flaw, and its 'workaround' section is largely moot since the reporter already applied and verified the fix themselves. X's added focus on regression-test coverage gaps is more useful to the actual thread's underlying goal (preventing recurrence) than Y's redundant verification instructions.
